servinfo.title: Time Lag Test
servinfo.ptxt: This test is performed to check conditions of clutch, brake and fluid pressure. “Time lag” means time elapsed since select lever is shifted with engine idling till shock is felt.

s1.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC.
Select “Data list” mode on scan tool.
Drive vehicle so that CVT fluid temperature reaches normal operating temperature (60 to 80 °C (140 to 176 °F)).

attention3.ptxt: Engine coolant temperature rises when revving engine in P or N range, but CVT fluid temperature does not rise in P or N range. Be sure to drive vehicle in order to raise CVT fluid temperature.

s1.ptxt: Apply parking brake and block wheels.
With stopwatch ready, shift select lever from N to D range and measure time from that moment till shock is felt.
Similarly measure time lag by shifting select lever from N to R range.

item.ptxt: When repeating this test, be sure to wait at least 1 minute after select lever is shifted back to N range.
Engine should be warmed up fully for this test.
Repeat test 3 times and take average of those data for final time lag data.

condition.ptxt: N → D time lag exceeds specification
N → R time lag exceeds specification

ps.ptxt: Broken reverse brake piston oil seal
